text: Oh, verily, it is they, they who are spreading corruption but they perceive it not?
the truth from thy Sustainer! Be not, then, among the doubters:
Verily, nothing on earth or in the heavens is hidden from God.
[This is] the truth from thy Sustainer; be not, then, among the doubters!
And pay heed unto God and the Apostle, so that you might be graced with mercy.
and indeed guide them onto a straight way.
nay, God exalted him unto Himself - and God is indeed almighty, wise.
Verily, that [reckoning] which you are promised is bound to come, and you cannot elude it!
[And God] replied: "Verily, thou shalt be among those who are granted a respite."
And he swore unto them, "Verily, I am of those who wish you well indeed!"
And Moses said: "O Pharaoh! Verily, I am an apostle from the Sustainer of all the worlds,
who wants to drive you out of your land! " [Said Pharaoh:] "What, then, do you advise?"
Answered [Pharaoh]: "Yes; and, verily, you shall be among those who are near unto me."
And thus were they vanquished there and then, and became utterly humiliated.
They answered: "Verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn-
And thereupon We made you their successors on earth, so that We might behold how you act.
Verily, God does not do the least wrong unto men, but it is men who wrong themselves.
AND, INDEED, We sent Moses with Our messages and a manifest authority [from Us]
And be patient in adversity: for, verily,. God does not fail to requite the doers of good!
They answered: "By God! Thou art indeed still lost in thy old aberration!"
AND, INDEED, We have created man out of sounding clay, out of dark-slime transmuted
Answered He: "Verily, so be it: thou shalt be among those who are granted respite
Verily, in all this there are messages indeed for those who can read the signs:
Verily, herein lies a message indeed for all who believe [in God].
AND, [likewise,] indeed, the people of Al-Hijr gave the lie to [Our] message-bearers:
verily, thy Sustainer is the all-knowing Creator of all things!
verily, We shall suffice thee against all who [now] deride [this message - all]
for, verily, God is with those who are conscious of Him and are doers of good withal!
and, verily, [in time] We shall reduce all that is on it to barren dust!
and in time she conceived him, and then she withdrew with him to a far-off place.
Indeed, [by this assertion] you have brought forth something monstrous,
indeed, He has full cognizance of them, and has numbered them with [unfailing] numbering;
"[And now] go thou unto Pharaoh: for, verily, he has transgressed all bounds of equity.
so that they might fully understand my speech,
so that [together] we might abundantly extol Thy limitless glory
Verily, Thou seest all that is within us!"
Answered He: "Fear not! Verily, I shall be with you two, hearing and seeing [all].
[But] We said: "Fear not! Verily, it is thou who shalt prevail!
Said [Moses]: "What, then, didst thou have in view, O Samaritan?"
[so that] thou wilt see no curve thereon, and no ruggedness.
And they could only cry: "Oh, woe unto us! Verily, we were wrongdoers!"
Said he: "Indeed, you and your forefathers have obviously gone astray!"
NOW, INDEED, We create man out of the essence of clay,
and then We cause him to remain as a drop of sperm in [the wombs] firm keeping,
And then, behold! after all this, you are destined to die;
and then, behold! you shall be raised from the dead on Resurrection Day.
and, indeed, if you pay heed to a mortal like your­selves, you will surely be the losers!
Far-fetched, far-fetched indeed is what you are promised!
[And God] would say: "After a little while they will surely be smitten with remorse!"
Verily, [only] they who stand in reverent awe of their Sustainer,
And, verily, thou callest them onto a straight way -
[And they will reply: "Unto God." Say: "Will you not, then, bethink yourselves [of Him]?"
verily, how evil an abode and a station!" -;
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
[Pharaoh] answered: "Produce it, then, if thou art a man of truth!"
who wants to drive you out of your land by his sorcery. What, then, do you advise?"
so that we might follow [in the footsteps of] the sorcerers if it is they who prevail?"
[But] then Moses threw his staff - and lo! it swallowed up all their deceptions.
They answered: "No harm [canst thou do to us]: verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn!
but they are indeed filled with hatred of us
seeing that we are, verily, a nation united, fully prepared against danger -
He replied: Nay indeed! My Sustainer is with me, [and] He will guide me!"
and then We caused the others to drown.
And yet, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
and who will cause me to die and then will bring me back to life -
"And forgive my father - for, verily, he is among those who have gone astray -
Would that we had a second chance [in life], so that we could be among the believers!"
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!
Said they: "Indeed, if thou desist not, O Noah, thou wilt surely be stoned to death!"
and then We caused those who stayed behind to drown.
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, con­scious of God, and pay heed unto me!
"Be, then, conscious of God and pay heed unto me:
for, verily, I fear lest suffering befall you on an awesome day!"
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!
"Be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me,
But they cruelly slaughtered her - and then they had cause to regret it:
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
Behold, I am an apostle [sent by Him] to you, [and therefore] worthy of your trust:
be, then, conscious of God, and pay heed unto me!
Cause, then, fragments of the sky to fall down upon us, if thou art a man of truth!"
But, verily, thy Sustainer - He alone - is almighty, a dispenser of grace!
upon thy heart, [O Muhammad] so that thou mayest be among those who preach
and then they will exclaim, "Could we have a respite?"
Do they, then, [really] wish that Our chas­tisement be hastened on?
verily, [even] from hearing it are they utterly debarred!
for, verily, He alone is all-hearing, all-knowing!
[And God spoke thus:] "O Moses! Verily I alone am God, the Almighty, the Wise!"
and, verily, it is a guidance and a grace unto all who believe [in it].
then He causes him to be begotten out of the essence of a humble fluid;
[for] there and then were the believers tried, and shaken with a shock severe.
verily, thou art indeed one of God's message-bearers,
Said [the apostles]: "Our Sustainer knows that we have indeed been sent unto you;
and so, behold, I would have indeed, most obviously, lost myself in error!
Verily, most surely, your God is One -
and perhaps also our forebears of old?"
Say: "Yea, indeed - and most abject will you then be!" -
and halt them [there]!" [And then,] behold, they shall be asked,
And, verily, on that Day they all will share in their common suffering.
and would say, "Shall we, then, give up our deities at the bidding of a mad poet?"
Behold, you will indeed taste grievous suffering [in the life to come],
But then, [O my friends in paradise,] is it [really] so that we are not to die
Verily, this - this indeed - is the triumph supreme!"
For the like of this, then, let them labour, those who labour [in God's way]!
Verily, We have caused it to be a trial for evildoers:
Thus, indeed, most of the people of old went astray before them,
[and so We saved him and those who followed him] and then We caused the others to drown.
What, then, do you think of the Sustainer of all the worlds?"
Then he cast a glance at the stars,
and said, "Verily, I am sick [at heart]!"
And then he fell upon them, smiting them with his right hand.
for, behold, all this was indeed a trial, clear in itself.
THUS, INDEED, did We bestow Our favour upon Moses and Aaron;
and succoured them, so that [in the end] it was they who achieved victory.
AND, BEHOLD, Elijah [too] was indeed one of Our message-bearers
AND, BEHOLD, Lot was indeed one of Our mes­sage-bearers;
and, verily, [to this day] you pass by the remnants of their dwellings at morning-time
and by night. Will you not, then, use your reason?
AND, BEHOLD, Jonah was indeed one of Our mes­sage-bearers
And then they cast lots, and he was the one who lost;
And [then] We sent him [once again] to [his people,] a hundred thousand [souls] or more:
Oh, verily, it is out of their own [inclination to] falsehood that some people assert,
"God has begotten [a son]"; and, verily, they are lying [too, when they say],
Will you not, then, bethink yourselves?
Produce, then, that divine writ of yours, if you are speaking the truth!
for, verily, neither you [blasphemers] nor the objects of your worship
and, verily, we too are ranged [before Him in worship];
and, verily, we too extol His limitless glory!"
AND, INDEED, they [who deny the truth] have always been wont to say,
that, verily, they - they indeed - would be succoured,
and that, verily, Our hosts - they indeed - would [in the end] be victorious!
Do they, then, [really] wish that Our chas­tisement be hastened on?
And We streng­thened his dominion, and bestowed upon him wisdom and sagacity in judgment.
And, behold, in Our sight they were indeed among the elect, the truly good!
this, verily, shall be Our provision [for you], with no end to it!
This, [then, for them -] so let them taste it: burning despair and ice-cold darkness
Answered He: "Verily, so [be it:] thou shalt be among those who are granted respite
[And God] said: "This, then, is the truth! And this truth do I state:
THUS, INDEED, did We send Moses with Our mes­sages and a manifest authority [from Us]
Hence, verily, it is unto Him that we must always turn."
And let not Satan bars you [from it] - for, verily, he is your open foe!
O our Sustainer, relieve us of suffering, for, verily, we [now] believe [in Thee]!"
And [God said]: "Go thou forth with My servants by night, for you will surely be pursued;
Thus it was. And [then] We made another people heirs [to what they had left],
And, indeed, We delivered the children of Israel from the shameful suffering
and indeed, We chose them knowingly above all other people,
[Now,] behold, these [people] say indeed:
So then, bring forth our forefathers [as witnesses], if what you claim is true!"
Verily, [in the life to come] the tree of deadly fruit
then pour over his head the anguish of burning despair!
[As against this -] verily, the God-conscious will find themselves in a state secure,
Will they not, then, ponder over this Qur'an? - or are there locks upon their hearts?
VERILY, [O Muhammad,] We have laid open before thee a manifest victory,
who has set up another deity beside God: cast him, then, cast him into suffering severe!"
Verily, it is We who grant life and deal death; and with Us will be all journeys' end
Verily, that which you are promised is true indeed,
and, verily, judgment is bound to come!
Verily, [O men,] you are deeply at variance as to what to believe:
just as [there are signs thereof] within your own selves: can you not, then, see?
Then he turned quietly to his household, and brought forth a fat [roasted] calf,
Turn, then, away from them, and thou shalt incur no blame;
Woe, then, on that Day to all who give the lie to the truth -
Was it, then, a delusion or is it that you failed to see [its truth]?
Say thou: "Wait, [then,] hopefully; behold, I, too, shall hopefully wait with you!"
and then drew near, and came close,
will you, then, contend with him as to what he saw?
And, indeed, he saw him a second time
HAVE YOU, then, ever considered [what you are worshipping in] Al-Lat and Al-Uzza,
and that [therefore] it is within His power to bring about a second life;
and then covered them from sight forever.
About which, then, of thy Sustainer's powers canst thou [still] remain in doubt?
And, indeed, abiding suffering did befall them early on the morrow:
Or do they say, "We are a group united, [and therefore] bound to prevail"?
so that you [too, O men,] might never transgress the measure [of what is right]:
weigh, therefore, [your deeds] with equity, and cut not the measure short!
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
Which, then, of your Sustain­er's powers can you disavow?
He has given freedom to the two great bodies of water, so that they might meet:
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?
which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow? -
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow? -
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow? -
[companions] whom neither man nor invisible being will have touched ere then.
Which, then, of your Sustainer's powers can you disavow?

so that they become as scat­tered dust -
[on that Day,] then, shall you be [divided into] three kinds.
and perhaps, too, our forebears of old?"
Say: "Verily, those of olden times and those of later times
will indeed be gathered together at an appointed time on a Day known [only to God]:
and then, verily, O you who have gone astray and called the truth a lie,
you will indeed have to taste of the tree of deadly fruit,
WE who have created you, [O men:] why, then, do you not accept the truth?
"Verily, we are ruined!
Extol, then, the limitless glory of thy Sus­tainer's mighty name!
and, behold, this is indeed a most solemn affirmation, if you but knew it!
Why, then, when [the last breath] comes up to the throat [of a dying man],
why, then, if [you think that] you are not truly dependent [on Us],
Verily, this is indeed the truth of truths!
Extol, then, the limitless glory of thy Sus­tainer's mighty name!
God - there is no deity save Him! In God then let the believers place their trust.
for, behold, thou keepest indeed to a sublime way of life;
they would like thee to be soft [with them], so that they might be soft [with thee].
so that by the morrow it became barren and bleak.
- [and then,] "Nay, but we have been rendered destitute!"
They answered: "Limitless in His glory is our Sustainer! Verily, we were doing wrong!"
- and then they turned upon one another with mutual reproaches.
[In the end] they said: "Oh, woe unto us! Verily, we did behave outrageously!
Behold, this [Qur'an] is indeed the [inspired] word of a noble apostle,
We would indeed have seized him by his right hand,
and would indeed have cut his life-vein.
And, verily, this [Qur'an] is a reminder to all the God-conscious!
for, verily, it is truth absolute!
Extol, then the limitless glory of thy Sustainer's mighty name!
Therefore, [O believer,] endure all adversity with goodly patience:
and of whoever [else] lives on earth, all of them - so that he could but save himself.
But nay! Verily, all [that awaits him] is a raging flame,
VERILY, man is born with a restless disposition.
and [then] He will bring you forth [from It] in resurrection.
so that you might walk thereon on spacious paths.'"
This, verily, is a reminder: let him who wills, then set out on a way to his Sustainer!
Nay, verily, it is against Our messages that he knowingly, stubbornly sets himself
and then he looks [around for new arguments],
and then he frowns and glares,
WHAT, THEN, is amiss with them that they turn away from all admonition
Nay, verily, this is an admonition -
Yea indeed, We are able to make whole his very finger-tips!
and then, behold, it will be for Us to make its meaning clear.
and then went arrogantly back to what he had come from.
DOES MAN, then, think that he is to be left to himself to go about at will?
Is not He, then; able to bring the dead back to life?
and then storming on with a tempest's force!
and then giving forth a reminder,
BEHOLD, all that you are told to expect will surely come to pass.
which We then let remain in [the womb's] firm keeping
In what other tiding, then, will they, after this, believe?
so that We might bring forth thereby grain, and herbs,
[But,] verily for the God-conscious there is supreme fulfilment in store:
[And yet,] some say, "What! Are we indeed to be restored to our former state -
[And] they add, "That, then, would be a return with loss!"
and then, lo, they will be fully awakened [to the truth]!
"Go unto Pharaoh - for, verily, he has transgressed all bounds of what is right -
and then he gathered [his great ones], and called [unto his people],
In this, behold, there is a lesson indeed for all who stand in awe [of God].
Yet for all thou didst know, [O Muhammad,] he might perhaps have grown in purity,
NAY, VERILY, these [messages] are but a reminder:
and then makes it easy for him to go through life;
and then, if it be His will, He shall raise him again to life,
Let man, then, consider [the sources of] his food:
and then We cleave the earth [with new growth], cleaving it asunder,
behold, this [divine writ] is indeed the [inspired] word of a noble apostle,
Whither, then, will you go?
And yet, verily, there are ever-watchful forces over you,
Behold, [in the life to come] the truly virtuous: will indeed be in bliss,
NAY, VERILY, the record of the wicked is indeed [set down] in a mode inescapable!
Nay, verily, from [the grace of] their Sustainer shall they on that Day be debarred;
NAY, VERILY - the record of the truly virtuous is [set down] in a mode most lofty!
Behold, [in the life to come] the truly virtuous will indeed be in bliss:
Yea indeed! His Sustainer did see all that was in him!
What, then, is amiss with them that they will not believe [in a life to come]?
and [then bethink thyself of] the promised Day,
VERILY, thy Sustainer's grip is exceedingly strong!
LET MAN, then, observe out of what he has been created:
BEHOLD, this [divine writ] is indeed a word that between truth and falsehood,
Verily, [all] this has indeed been [said] in the earlier revelations -
and verily, It is for Us to call them to account.
and therefore thy Sustainer let loose upon them a scourge of suffering:
for, verily, thy Sustainer is ever on the watch!
enter, then, together with My [other true] servants -
Verily, We have created man into [a life of] pain, toil and trial.
Does he, then, think that no one has power over him?
Does he, then, think that no one sees him?
To a happy state shall indeed attain he who causes this [self] to grow in purity,
Verily, [O men,] you aim at most divergent ends!
BEHOLD, it is indeed for Us to grace [you] with guidance;
he that spends his possessions [on others] so that he might grow in purity -
and such, indeed, shall in time be well-pleased.
Therefore, the orphan shalt thou never wrong,
verily, with every hardship comes ease!
Verily, We create man in the best conformation;
What, then, [O man,] could henceforth cause thee to give the lie to this moral law?
Nay, verily, man becomes grossly overweening
Does he, then, not know that God sees [all]?
Nay, if he desist not, We shall most surely drag him down upon his forehead
and then let him summon [to his aid] the counsels of his own [spurious] wisdom,
and to this, behold, he [himself] bears witness indeed:
for, verily, to the love of wealth is he most ardently devoted.
And then, he whose weight [of good deeds] is heavy in the balance
In the end you will indeed, most surely, behold it with the eye of certainty:
Verily, man is bound to lose himself
verily, it will close in upon them
SO THAT the Quraysh might remain secure,
Let them, therefore, worship the Sustainer of this Temple;
Woe, then, unto those praying ones
Verily, he that hates thee has indeed been cut off [from all that is good]!
